content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all four pending amendments for planned-elsewhere reconciliation correctness. Amendment 1: Changed the completion_round extraction in rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal (service.rs) from .as_u64().map(|v| v as u32) to .as_u64().and_then(|v| u32::try_from(v).ok()), avoiding silent truncation of values above u32::MAX. Amendment 2: Verified via comprehensive audit that all consumers of MilestoneJournalPort::read_journal use equality-based filtering (== MilestoneEventType::...) and no consumer relies on deserialization failure as a corruption signal. The Unknown variant's existing doc comment ('Consumers should skip Unknown events') is sufficient documentation — no code change needed. Amendment 3: Fixed the latest-round logic in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success and reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings. Previously, the authoritative 'latest round' was derived from journal mapping rows, which broke when the latest final-review round fixed/rejected all PE findings (writing zero PE rows). Now reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings returns the authoritative max completion_round from the aggregates themselves (the highest round among all FinalReview StageAggregate payloads for this run), and verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success uses that as the primary source of truth for filtering. If no aggregates exist, falls back to journal-derived max_round. Amendment 4: Added extract_pe_bead_ids() in task_prompt_contract.rs that parses the 'Already Planned Elsewhere' section of the canonical prompt to extract allowed bead IDs from bullet lines. In record_planned_elsewhere_amendments (engine.rs), the prompt is now loaded via the project record's prompt_reference and the allowed set is extracted. Any mapped_to_bead_id not in the allowed set is rejected with a warning log, preventing hallucinated but real bead IDs from bypassing the restart/fix loop. If the prompt cannot be read or has no PE section, validation is skipped (best-effort).

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendment 1: Changed completion_round extraction in rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal (service.rs) from .as_u64().map(|v| v as u32) to .as_u64().and_then(|v| u32::try_from(v).ok()), using a checked cast instead of silent truncation.
2. [Completed] Amendment 2: Comprehensive audit of all MilestoneJournalPort::read_journal consumers confirmed that all use equality-based filtering (== MilestoneEventType::...) and none rely on deserialization failure for Unknown event types. The existing doc comment on the Unknown variant is sufficient documentation. No code change needed.
3. [Completed] Amendment 3a: Changed reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings return type from Vec<PlannedElsewhereMapping> to (Vec<PlannedElsewhereMapping>, Option<u32>). The second element is the authoritative max completion_round from all FinalReview StageAggregate payloads for this run — the source of truth even when the latest round has zero PE amendments.
4. [Completed] Amendment 3b: In verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success, replaced the journal-derived max_round filter with an authoritative_max_round from the aggregates. Falls back to journal-derived max if no aggregates found. This correctly excludes stale earlier-round PE mappings even when the latest round writes zero PE rows.
5. [Completed] Amendment 3c: Updated the reconstruct_pe_mappings_deduplicates_by_round_latest_wins test to destructure the new tuple return type and assert authoritative_max_round == Some(5).
6. [Completed] Amendment 4a: Added extract_pe_bead_ids() public function in task_prompt_contract.rs that finds the '## Already Planned Elsewhere' section in a canonical prompt, iterates bullet lines, and extracts the first whitespace-delimited token (the bead ID) from each into a HashSet.
7. [Completed] Amendment 4b: In record_planned_elsewhere_amendments (engine.rs), added prompt loading via FileSystem::live_project_root + project_record.prompt_reference, extraction of allowed PE bead IDs via extract_pe_bead_ids, and validation of each mapped_to_bead_id against the allowed set. Non-matching IDs are rejected with a warning log and the amendment is skipped.
8. [Completed] Ran cargo fmt, cargo test (758 passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy (no new warnings from changed files), cargo fmt --check (clean).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 758 passed, 0 failed across all test suites (lib, integration, unit, doc-tests)
- cargo fmt --check: exit 0, no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: no new warnings from any changed file (service.rs, success_reconciliation.rs, engine.rs, task_prompt_contract.rs); all 19 warnings are pre-existing in unrelated files
- Amendment 1 verified: completion_round extraction at service.rs:2491-2494 now uses .and_then(|v| u32::try_from(v).ok()) — values above u32::MAX produce None instead of being silently truncated
- Amendment 2 verified: comprehensive audit of all read_journal consumers confirmed equality-based filtering throughout. No consumer uses exhaustive match on MilestoneEventType. Unknown events are naturally skipped by all == checks. The apply_journal_write_ops path preserves Unknown events verbatim during journal rewrites.
- Amendment 3 verified: reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings now returns (Vec, Option<u32>) where the Option is the max completion_round from aggregates. verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success uses authoritative_max_round from aggregates as primary source, falling back to journal-derived max. When the latest round has zero PE amendments, its round number still filters out stale earlier-round mappings.
- Amendment 4 verified: extract_pe_bead_ids parses '- bead-id (title)' bullet lines from the Already Planned Elsewhere section. record_planned_elsewhere_amendments loads the prompt, extracts allowed IDs, and skips any mapped_to not in the set with a warning log. Empty allowed set (prompt unreadable) skips validation for backward compatibility.
- Files modified: src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs (checked u32 cast), src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs (authoritative round from aggregates, updated test), src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs (PE bead ID validation), src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs (extract_pe_bead_ids function)

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19 from code outside this change) cause cargo clippy -- -D warnings to fail; none are introduced by this change.
- One flaky integration test (checkpoint_test::worktree_adapter_includes_*) occasionally fails due to git timing; unrelated to this change.
- The extract_pe_bead_ids function parses bead IDs from bullet lines using the format '- {id} ({title}) - {relationship}'. If the prompt format changes (e.g., numbered lists or different markdown), the parser would fail to extract IDs and validation would be skipped (allowed set empty). This is acceptable as a best-effort safeguard.
- When the prompt file cannot be read (e.g., deleted or moved), validation is skipped and all mapped_to_bead_id values are accepted. This preserves backward compatibility and avoids blocking reconciliation on filesystem errors.
- The authoritative_max_round from aggregates only covers rounds that produced a FinalReview StageAggregate payload. If a round completes without producing an aggregate (hypothetical edge case), it would not be reflected in the authoritative round.
